description_long: This object is a pair of pliers with dark green, ergonomic handles and a metallic, silver-colored head. The handles are slightly curved for a comfortable grip and appear to be coated with a non-slip material. The jaws are slender and pointed, resembling those of needle-nose pliers, and are joined by a central pivot mechanism. The metallic jaws have a smooth finish and taper to a fine tip, suitable for precision work such as gripping, bending, or cutting small wires. The overall construction suggests durability and utility for tasks in garages, workshops, or urban maintenance settings. No visible branding or logos are present on the tool.
description: A pair of needle-nose pliers with dark green ergonomic handles and a metallic, pointed jaw for precision gripping and cutting tasks.
description_view_0: Front view showing the two green handles and the pointed metallic jaws facing forward.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the slim, elongated shape of the handles and the thin, tapered jaws.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the handles and the closed alignment of the jaws.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile showing the full length of the handles and the sharp, narrow jaw tips.
attribute_handle_type: Curved, ergonomic handles with a likely rubberized or plastic coating for improved grip.
attribute_jaw_design: Needle-nose, slender and pointed jaws with a smooth gripping surface, suitable for precision tasks.
attribute_pivot_mechanism: Fixed central pivot, allowing standard opening and closing motion without adjustable jaw width.
attribute_grip_pattern: Smooth with possible subtle texturing for grip, but no pronounced ribbing or knurling visible.
